elections.note: Numerator from the Registrar's official 'Unofficial Election Night Final' report (dated 11/07/18 10:47am; header states 'Outstanding Ballots to be Processed: 490,000'). Countywide 'Times Counted' = 662,718 ballots. This is the end-of-election-night cumulative total reported the morning after, not the literal 8pm first tranche. 662,718/1,173,924 = 56.5%. Traditional neighborhood polling-place model (2,136 precincts) — pre Voter's Choice Act vote centers.

Derived end-of-election-night/morning-after count: certified 1,627,753 minus ~376,000 ballots reported still outstanding (NBC7, day after) = ~1,251,753 counted; cross-checked by ~64.4% election-night turnout x 1,949,073 registered = ~1,255,000. ~76.9% of certified. First Voter's Choice Act presidential general; the 8pm first report unusually also included polling-place precinct ballots plus the very large pre-election-day mail return, so the election-night share was high. Literal 8pm first-tranche count not sourceable (Wayback blocked).

San Diego Union-Tribune / Del Mar Times: by the morning after the election the Registrar had released 565,982 ballots counted (= 29.4% of ~1.9M registered), with roughly a half-million still to count. End-of-election-night cumulative total reported morning after, not the literal 8pm tranche. 565,982/1,043,490 = 54.2%. First general using e-pollbooks; vote-center (VCA) model with fewer in-person locations and less frequent election-night updates.

As of ~10am Wednesday (morning after), the Registrar reported 49.2% of 1,983,767 registered had been counted = ~976,013 ballots, with ~590,000 still to process (per San Diego Patch). End-of-election-night running total, not the literal 8pm first tranche (Wayback blocked). 976,013/1,503,018 = 64.9%. First general with both e-pollbooks and automated signature verification; vote-center (VCA) model.
